title: Quiz: Second-Quantized Hamiltonian
description: Test your understanding of the molecular Hamiltonian in second quantization

questions.question: What is the second-quantized form of the one-electron Hamiltonian?
questions.explanation: The one-electron Hamiltonian is Ĥ₁ = Σₚₑ hₚₑ c†ₚcq, summing over all orbital pairs p,q with one-electron integrals hₚₑ as coefficients.

questions.question: Why is there a factor of ½ in the two-electron Hamiltonian Ĥ₂ = ½Σ(pq|rs)c†ₚc†ᵣcₛcq?

options.text: To avoid double counting electron pairs

questions.explanation: The factor of ½ avoids double counting. When summing over all indices p,q,r,s, each electron pair (i,j) is counted twice (as i-j and j-i), so we divide by 2.

questions.question: What is the scaling of two-electron integrals with system size M?
questions.explanation: There are four orbital indices in (pq|rs), giving O(M⁴) integrals. With 8-fold symmetry, this reduces to O(M⁴/8) unique integrals.

questions.question: What is the relationship between chemist's (pq|rs) and physicist's ⟨pq|rs⟩ notation?
questions.explanation: The notations differ in index ordering. Chemist's notation groups by electron: (pq|rs) = ⟨φₚφq|1/r₁₂|φᵣφₛ⟩. Physicist's notation: ⟨pq|rs⟩ = (pr|qs).

questions.question: The Hamiltonian matrix element between two Slater determinants that differ by more than two orbitals is always zero.
questions.explanation: True. The Hamiltonian contains only one- and two-body operators. By Slater-Condon rules, matrix elements vanish when determinants differ by more than two orbitals.

questions.question: What is the computational cost of the AO to MO integral transformation?
questions.explanation: Transforming four-index integrals one index at a time costs O(M⁵). The first transformation (μν|λσ) → (pν|λσ) is the bottleneck.

questions.question: For the HF ground state, what is ⟨Φ|Ĥ|Φₐⁱ⟩ (single excitation)?
questions.explanation: ⟨Φ|Ĥ|Φₐⁱ⟩ = Fₐᵢ. For the converged HF ground state, Fₐᵢ = 0 (Brillouin's theorem), but the general formula gives the Fock matrix element.

questions.question: Which are properties of one-electron integrals hₚₑ? (Select all)
questions.explanation: One-electron integrals include kinetic energy and nuclear attraction. They are Hermitian. Electron-electron repulsion is in two-electron integrals, not one-electron.
